findings.evidence: PR line 59 defines `LiftCondition (hp : IsCoveringMap p) ...`; Mathlib’s induced map API is for continuous maps (`mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/FundamentalGroupoid/FundamentalGroup.lean:80` `def map (f : C(X, Y))`, `:86` `def mapOfEq`), while the covering theorem separately assumes `cov : IsCoveringMap p` and takes the range inclusion at `mathlib/Mathlib/Topology/Homotopy/Lifting.lean:490-493`.
findings.fix: Define the condition for `q : C(E, X)`, e.g. `def LiftCondition (q : C(E, X)) (f : C(A, X)) (a₀ : A) (e₀ : E) (he : q e₀ = f a₀) : Prop := (FundamentalGroup.map f a₀).range ≤ (FundamentalGroup.mapOfEq q he).range`, then specialize it with `q := ⟨p, hp.continuous⟩` when applying the covering theorem.
findings.issue: `LiftCondition` takes `hp : IsCoveringMap p`, but the condition itself only uses `hp.continuous` to form `FundamentalGroup.mapOfEq`; covering is a hypothesis of the lifting theorem, not of the subgroup-inclusion predicate.

summary: The named condition is stated one level too specifically: it requires a covering-map proof even though the subgroup inclusion only needs a continuous map on the total space.
